item.title: ULAB, ELEL partner to boost student skills
item.description: The University of Liberal Arts Bangladesh signed a memorandum of understanding with e-Learning & Earning Ltd, a leading IT training and freelancing solutions institute, on ULAB campus in Dhaka on Monday....

item.title: Coast Guard rescues 22 in separate incidents
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard rescued 22 people in two separate maritime incidents off Cox’s Bazar and Chattogram within two days....

item.title: Iran to hold talks with Qatar on frozen assets
item.description: Iran will hold talks with mediator Qatar on Wednesday regarding its frozen assets, the Iranian foreign ministry said, denying that any meeting with the United States was planned in the Gulf state, where delegations from both sides were expected....

item.title: UN calls for food, shelter to help Venezuela quake survivors
item.description: Tens of thousands of people urgently need food and shelter in Venezuela due to the devastation from two huge earthquakes that killed more than 1,700 and injured 5,000, the UN said on Tuesday, as doctors warned of potential outbreaks of disease....

item.title: HSC, equivalent exams start tomorrow
item.description: The Higher Secondary Certificate and its equivalent examinations are going to start tomorrow across Bangladesh....

item.title: BB cuts private credit target, keeps repo rate unchanged
item.description: Bangladesh Bank has kept its policy repo rate unchanged at 10 per cent for the third consecutive monetary policy while sharply lowering its private sector credit growth target to 6.8 per cent for the first half of FY27, amid weak investment and slowing business activity......

item.title: Govt plans steps to sustain exports
item.description: The government has laid emphasis on product and market diversification, retention of existing export destinations, exploration of new markets and closer monitoring of Bangladesh’s commercial missions abroad to ensure sustainable growth in the country’s export earnings......

item.title: BEPZA attracts investments of $260m in H1
item.description: Total 12 foreign companies signed investment deals worth $259.52 million with the Bangladesh Export Processing Zones Authority in the first half or January-June period of the calendar year 2026, according to the data from the investment agency......

item.title: BB introduces one-time exit for defaulters
item.description: Bangladesh Bank has introduced a one-time special exit facility for defaulting borrowers, allowing banks to waive both accrued and unaccrued interest if borrowers repay their entire outstanding liabilities in a single payment......

item.title: A-Challan must for all govt receipts from today
item.description: The government has made A-Challan mandatory for depositing all public revenues and other government receipts from July 1, 2026, aiming to bring every receipt into the Treasury Single Account in real time, end manual challans and strengthen public cash management......

item.title: 20 partner orgs get Robi Elite recognition
item.description: Robi Axiata PLC on Tuesday honoured 20 organisations with ‘Robi Elite Partners Excellence Award 2026’ in recognition of their outstanding contribution to customer engagement, service excellence and value creation within the Robi Elite ecosystem during the first half of 2026......

item.title: VEON team meets with BB governor
item.description: A high-level delegation from the VEON Group, led by chairman Augie Fabela, on Tuesday met Bangladesh Bank governor Md Mostaqur Rahman to discuss expanding the company’s presence in Bangladesh’s rapidly growing digital financial services sector......

item.title: Nat’l minorities demand constitutional recognition
item.description: Members of the Santal and other national minority communities on Tuesday renewed their demand for constitutional recognition as indigenous peoples, protection of their ancestral land and the formation of a separate land commission for plainsland national minority communities....

item.title: Adani to sell stake in India port to MSC for $1.4b
item.description: Indian conglomerate Adani Group said Tuesday that Europe’s MSC will buy a 49 per cent stake in its deep-water transshipment port in southern India for $1.4 billion......

item.title: Myanmar denies ASEAN request for Suu Kyi meeting
item.description: Myanmar’s authorities have denied a request for ASEAN’s special envoy to meet its deposed and detained democratic leader Aung San Suu Kyi, an administration spokeswoman said Tuesday....

item.title: Crimes drop compared to past 15yrs: home minster
item.description: Overall crime, including murder and robbery, has declined in the country compared with the last 10 to 15 years, home minister Salahuddin Ahmed told parliament on Tuesday....

item.title: Sri Lanka cuts fuel prices, inflation hits 3-year high
item.description: Sri Lanka cut petrol and diesel prices by up to six per cent on Tuesday, its first reduction since the Middle East war triggered a surge in energy prices in the import-dependent nation......

item.title: Bangladeshi FundedNext tops Deloitte Fintech rankings
item.description: FundedNext, founded by two Bangladeshi entrepreneurs, has topped Deloitte’s Technology Fast 50 fintech rankings for the Middle East and Cyprus, highlighting Bangladesh’s growing presence in the global technology sector......

item.title: ONE Bank launches Bangla QR awareness campaign
item.description: ONE Bank PLC has recently launched an awareness campaign on Bangla QR, Bangladesh’s national interoperable QR payment system, said a press release......

item.title: Untaxed fund amnesty ends, vigilance must begin
item.description: THE passage of the Finance Bill 2026 by the parliament on June 29 approving the financial measures for the 2026–27 financial year has come with some amendments to the national budget proposed on June 11. Worth noting among the measures is that the controversial provision to legalise undisclosed money has been done away with. The provision had remained an......

item.title: A university without research is no university
item.description: RESEARCH allocation in the budget of the University of Dhaka has traditionally been meagre. The situation has been deplorable this year as the senate on June 29 approved a budget of about Tk 10.33 billion for the 2026–27 financial year with no allocation for research. No allocation for research in the budget not only betrays the poor standing of the university, it also......Maps

item.title: Why Bangladesh needs a budget delivery framework
item.description: EVERY year, the national budget becomes one of the most closely watched policy events in Bangladesh. Newspapers dissect the figures, economists debate fiscal deficits, businesses examine tax measures, and citizens look for indications of how the coming year may affect inflation, employment, education, healthcare and social protection. The finance adviser or......

item.title: A forgotten founder of Dhaka University
item.description: PHILANTHROPIST Nawab Syed Nawab Ali Choudhury (1863–1929) tops the list of those who made significant contributions to bringing Dhaka University into existence. Others like Sir Nawab Salimullah (1871–1915) and AK Fazlul Huq (1873–1962) put great efforts in campaigning for it and are duly recognised. It is true that they were at the forefront of the campaign for......Politics

item.title: Rethinking humanity’s relationship with land
item.description: THE annual observance of Desertification and Drought Day on June 17 has passed, but the concerns it highlights remain as urgent as ever. Around the world, land degradation continues to undermine food security, livelihoods, economic stability and ecological resilience. Recognising the growing threat posed by desertification and drought, the United Nations General......

item.title: Koeman steps down as Netherlands coach after World Cup exit
item.description: Netherlands coach Ronald Koeman said on Tuesday he was stepping down after his team were knocked out of the World Cup by Morocco in the last 32....

item.title: Mbappe scores twice as France breeze past Sweden
item.description:
Kylian Mbappe scored twice and Michael Olise was in scintillating form as France beat Sweden 3-0 at the World Cup on Tuesday and set up a last-16 clash with Paraguay.
...

item.title: NASA vows to send ball to the Moon if US wins World Cup
item.description: NASA's chief on Tuesday tried to rally Team USA players in this year's World Cup tournament, promising to send a soccer ball to the Moon if they win it all. ...

item.title: Haaland hailed as 'greatest goalscorer' after more heroics
item.description: Erling Haaland was described as ‘devastating’ and ‘the greatest goalscorer in the world’ after pouncing for a dramatic late winner on Tuesday for Norway at the World Cup....

item.title: JCD holds candlelight vigil to honour July martyrs
item.description: The ruling BNP-backed student body held the event to honour the student sand citizens, who made supreme sacrifices in the student-led July 2024 mass uprising......

item.title: Portugal's Silva bides his time after being benched
item.description: Bernardo Silva said it was not his job to question the coach's decisions after being benched for the past two games as Portugal prepare for Thursday's World Cup showdown against Croatia....

item.title: Ancelotti shows Brazil his worth but concerns remain
item.description: Carlo Ancelotti kept his trademark cool to guide Brazil into the World Cup last 16 but defender Gabriel admits there is room for improvement as they attempt to defy the sceptics....

item.title: Six burnt in Karnaphuli fishing vessel blast
item.description: Six crew members sustained burn injuries after an explosion in the engine room of a fishing vessel triggered a fire while it was anchored on the Karnaphuli River in Chattogram on Tuesday....

item.title: Five Qatar crash victims buried
item.description: The five Bangladeshi expatriates killed in a road accident in Qatar on June 21 were laid to rest at their ancestral graveyards in Kanaighat, Sylhet, on Tuesday afternoon, following an emotional homecoming....

item.title: 16 forest officials hurt in attack during eviction drive
item.description: At least 16 forest officials were injured in an attack during a drive against unauthorised structures built on forest land in Gazipur’s Sreepur upazila on Tuesday....

item.title: One killed in Jhenaidah BNP factional clash
item.description: A man died on Monday night after sustaining critical injuries in a factional clash between two rival groups of the Bangladesh Nationalist Party at Shailkupa upazila of Jhenaidah....

item.title: NSU hosts Social Business Innovation Summit 2026
item.description: North South University, through its School of Business and Economics, in collaboration with Yunus Centre, on Tuesday organised the Social Business Innovation Summit 2026 at the NSU Auditorium, bringing together policymakers, academics, entrepreneurs and social business......

item.title: Chhatra Shakti announces programmes to mark July uprising anniv
item.description: Jatiya Chhatra Shakti, an associate organisation of the National Citizen Party, on Tuesday announced a month-long programme to mark the second anniversary of the July mass uprising with the theme ‘From July to rise, from rise to reconstruction’....

item.title: WIFE’S SUICIDE: Actor Alvi sent to jail after remand
item.description: A Dhaka court on Tuesday sent actor Md Neyamot Ullah Bhuiyan, popularly known as Zaher Alvi, to jail after the completion of his two-day remand in a case alleging that he had abetted his wife, Afra Ibnat Ikra, to commit suicide....City& Local Guides

item.title: SAU trains faculty on AI safety, data protection
item.description: Sher-e-Bangla Agricultural University on Tuesday organised a training workshop titled ‘Training Workshop on Data Protection, Safe and Secure Use of AI for SAU Faculties’ to promote the safe, responsible and effective use of artificial intelligence in education and research....

item.title: Haaland sends Norway into last-16 Brazil clash
item.description:
Erling Haaland scored a late winner as Norway set up a last-16 showdown with Brazil at the World Cup after taming the Ivory Coast 2-1 in Texas on Tuesday.
...

item.title: RCC announces Tk 1,010 crore grant-dependent budget
item.description: The Rajshahi City Corporation on Monday announced a proposed budget of Tk 1,009.66 crore for the 2026–27 financial year, with a significant portion of the allocation dependent on government grants, donor-assisted schemes and development projects, underscoring......

item.title: Budget remains unrealistic: Jamaat
item.description: The Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami, the main opposition in Jatiya Sangsad, on Tuesday criticised the approved national budget for the 2026–27 financial year as unplanned, opaque and unrealistic....

item.title: MPO teachers elected to UP can’t receive dual benefits: DSHE
item.description: The Directorate of Secondary and Higher Education has clarified that any teacher working in a private MPO-listed educational institution, if elected as a Union Parishad chairman or member, cannot receive both MPO benefits and the honorarium of an elected public representative simultaneously....
